summary: CRITICAL BUG FIX VERIFIED: Kit completeness calculation now correctly sums quantities instead of counting records. All test scenarios passed: non-unique items with quantity > 1, over-complete display, transfer updates, and damaged item exclusion.

minor.issue: Transfer event for quantity-tracked items doesn't automatically update item location. Only individual items are auto-updated.
minor.note: This is expected behavior - quantity transfers use /api/events/transfer-quantity endpoint

critical_code_review_comments: ✓ FIX VERIFIED: Kit completeness now uses .reduce() to sum quantities instead of .length to count records
✓ Non-unique items (IMUs, USB Hubs) with quantity > 1 display correctly (e.g., 5/5 not 1/5)
✓ Over-complete scenario works (e.g., 6/5 with '+1 excess' indicator)
✓ Transfer updates completeness correctly in both source and destination kits
✓ Damaged items are correctly excluded from completeness calculation
✓ Distribution tab correctly sums quantities (backend already uses qty)
✓ Code at lines 1043-1045 in Inventory.js: currentQuantity = matchingItems.reduce((sum, item) => sum + (parseInt(item.quantity) || 1), 0)
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up test items: TEST-IMU-QTY5, TEST-USB-HUB-1, TEST-GLOVE-LEFT-01, TEST-IMU-EXTRA-1, TEST-IMU-DAMAGE-TEST
context_for_next_testing_agent: Kit completeness bug fix verified. The fix changes quantity calculation from counting records (.length) to summing quantities (.reduce). All scenarios tested: adding items with qty > 1, over-complete display, transfer updates, and damaged item exclusion.
passed_tests: Kit Completeness: Non-unique item (IMU) with quantity=5 shows 5/5 Complete
Kit Completeness: Unique item (Glove Left) shows 1/1 Complete
Kit Completeness: Over-complete (6/5 IMUs) shows '+1 excess' indicator
Kit Completeness: Transfer updates source kit (6/5 → 5/5)
Kit Completeness: Transfer updates destination kit (0/5 → 1/5)
Kit Completeness: Damaged items excluded from count (7 total, 5 active = 5/5)
Distribution Tab: Shows correct quantities summed from items
Distribution Tab: Excludes damaged/lost items from count
features_verified: 1. Kit Completeness: Correctly sums quantity for non-unique items (IMUs, USB Hubs, etc.)
2. Kit Completeness: Works for unique items (Gloves, Laptops - each record counts as 1)
3. Kit Completeness: Updates after Add Item
4. Kit Completeness: Updates after Transfer Item
5. Kit Completeness: Updates after Report Damage (damaged items excluded)
6. Distribution Tab: Shows correct quantities (backend already uses qty)
7. Edge Case: Over-complete (e.g. 6/5 IMUs) displays correctly with excess indicator

bug_fix_details.impact: Non-unique items like IMUs now correctly show total quantity instead of record count
